chapters.title: New Bark Town & the First Errand
chapters.intro: Professor Elm has an errand for you: visit Mr. Pokémon, west of Cherrygrove City. The trip out is quiet — the trip back is not.

steps.text: Start a new game: choose your gender, set the current time and day, and pick your name (up to 7 characters; presets are Gold/Hiro/Taylor/Karl or Kris/Amanda/Juana/Jodi).

Downstairs, Mom hands you the Pokégear and sets its Clock — she asks the day of the week and about Daylight Saving Time. Day-of-week events (Berry trees, the Week Siblings, the Bug-Catching Contest) all hang off this clock.
In Elm's lab, choose your starter at level 5: Chikorita (Grass — harder early game), Cyndaquil (Fire — easiest), or Totodile (Water — most balanced). You may nickname it (up to 10 characters).
Take the Potion from Elm's aide, get Elm's phone number, and head out — Mr. Pokémon's house is north of Route 30, west of Cherrygrove City.

Cross Route 29 westward. The grass holds Pidgey, Rattata, Sentret, Hoothoot, and Hoppip (levels 2–3), but with no Poké Balls you can only walk past — your catching pass comes on the way back. Grab the Potion east of the northeastern grass patch; a Berry grows daily on the tree by the northwest hill.
In Cherrygrove City, let the Guide Gent on the east side show you around — he rewards you with the Map Card for the Pokégear. Heal at the Pokémon Center.

Head north on Route 30 toward Mr. Pokémon's house. The youngsters here are fought on the way back, after the Elm errand. For now, pick up the Antidote and the hidden Potion; Berry trees grow here daily.
At Mr. Pokémon's house in the northeastern clearing, receive the Mystery Egg. Professor Oak is here too — he gives you the Pokédex. Mr. Pokémon heals your team before you leave.
Heading back, a red-haired trainer ambushes you east of Cherrygrove City. He fights with the starter that beats yours, at level 5. Win for $300.
Back at Elm's lab, a police officer is investigating the break-in — a Pokémon was stolen. Give him your rival's name (up to 7 characters).

Hand the Mystery Egg to Elm; his aide gives you 5 Poké Balls — catching is unlocked, and Poké Marts sell them from here on. At home, Mom offers to save 25% of your battle earnings — her savings later buy Super Potions, Repels, a Moon Stone, and dolls.

chapters.title: Violet City
chapters.intro: Poké Balls in hand, the same roads are new again: catch on Route 29, fight the youngsters on Route 30, then take on Sprout Tower and Falkner — under the first Set-style level cap of the run.

steps.text: Back across Route 29, now catching: Pidgey, Rattata, Sentret, Hoothoot, and Hoppip (levels 2–3) live in the grass. Open your Pokédex here — every early catch counts (see the catch list for what's available before Falkner).

North through Route 30 again — the trainers fight you now: Youngster Joey (Rattata Lv. 4), Youngster Mikey (Pidgey Lv. 2, Rattata Lv. 4), Bug Catcher Don (2× Caterpie Lv. 3). Register Joey after the win — his rematch chain eventually rewards an HP Up. The grass here is open for catching too (encounter table has the details).

Route 31 connects Route 30 to Violet City; the cave mouth leads into Dark Cave (you lack the field moves for it now). Defeat Bug Catcher Wade and register him — he calls with Berries and healing items. Items: Potion near the Dark Cave sign, Poké Ball south of the pond, a Bitter Berry tree (daily). Grass: Caterpie, Weedle, Pidgey, Zubat, Poliwag, Bellsprout, Gastly, Hoothoot, Ledyba, Spinarak, Hoppip — catch a Bellsprout for the trade ahead.

Violet City has the first Gym, a Pokémon academy, and a trade: a local trainer exchanges your Bellsprout for an Onix — catch a Bellsprout on Route 31 first; Onix is a big help against Falkner (and the trade counts toward completing every in-game trade later).

Climb Sprout Tower. Sages Nico, Chow, and Edmond use 3× Bellsprout Lv. 3 each; Sages Jin and Neal use Bellsprout Lv. 6; Sage Troy has Bellsprout Lv. 7 and Hoothoot Lv. 7. Items on the way: Paralyze Heal, X Accuracy, Potion, Escape Rope.
The Elder, Sage Li, fights with 2× Bellsprout Lv. 7 (Vine Whip, Growth) and Hoothoot Lv. 10. Beat him for HM05 Flash and $320.

SET-STYLE CAP — Falkner: for 'Clipping the Pigeon's Wings', fight in the Set battle style, with Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 9 during the battle, and no Pack items. Check your levels before stepping in.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Falkner — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Gym trainers first: Bird Keeper Abe (Spearow Lv. 9) and Bird Keeper Rod (2× Pidgey Lv. 7). Rock-type moves and the traded Onix shine here.
Falkner: Pidgey Lv. 7 (Tackle, Mud-Slap) and Pidgeotto Lv. 9 (Tackle, Mud-Slap, Gust). Victory earns the Zephyr Badge (Attack boost, Flash usable in the field), TM31 Mud-Slap, and $900.

After the badge, Professor Elm's aide finds you in Violet City and hands over the Egg — carry it in your party; it hatches into Togepi after enough steps (showing the hatched Pokémon to Elm later earns an Everstone).

chapters.title: Ruins of Alph & Union Cave
chapters.intro: The road south of Violet City detours past ancient ruins where the walls watch back.

steps.text: Head south onto Route 32. A man north of the first grass patch gifts a Miracle Seed. Trainers on the land stretch: Youngster Albert (Rattata Lv. 6, Zubat Lv. 8), Picnicker Liz (Nidoran♀ Lv. 9), Camper Roland (Nidoran♂ Lv. 9), Bird Keeper Peter (2× Pidgey Lv. 6, Spearow Lv. 8). Along the pier: Fisher Henry (2× Poliwag Lv. 8), Fisher Justin (4× Magikarp Lv. 5–15), Fisher Ralph (Goldeen Lv. 10), Youngster Gordon (Wooper Lv. 10). Items: Repel, Super Potion, 2× Great Ball, Poison Barb. The Fishing Guru in the Pokémon Center near Union Cave hands out the Old Rod — Magikarp and Tentacool bite (Qwilfish during swarms). TM05 Roar on the northern hill needs Cut — that's a later pass.

Detour into the Ruins of Alph. Only the north chamber opens now: solve its Kabuto sliding puzzle to drop into the main hall and wake Unown forms A–K. Catch three different forms and the researcher upgrades your Pokédex with Unown Mode. Use an Escape Rope at the rear panel to reveal the secret room items: Berry, PSNCureBerry, Heal Powder, Energy Powder. The remaining puzzle chambers and hidden items need field moves you don't have — the ruins call you back much later.

Through Union Cave's first floor: Hiker Daniel (Onix Lv. 11), Hiker Russell (3× Geodude Lv. 4–8), Firebreather Bill (2× Koffing Lv. 6), PokéManiac Larry (Slowpoke Lv. 10), Firebreather Ray (Vulpix Lv. 9). Items: X Attack, Great Ball, Potion, Awakening. The northern B1F and the Surf-only B2F (with its Friday Lapras) wait for later passes.
Out the south exit onto rainy Route 33: Hiker Anthony (Geodude Lv. 11, Machop Lv. 11), a PSNCureBerry tree (daily), and Azalea Town ahead.

chapters.title: Azalea Town
chapters.intro: Team Rocket is back and farming Slowpoke tails. Azalea wants its well — and its Slowpoke — back.

steps.text: In Azalea Town, Kurt the Poké Ball craftsman storms off to deal with Team Rocket cutting off Slowpoke tails — he rushes into Slowpoke Well and falls in. Follow him.
Rescue the Slowpoke: beat the three Rocket Grunts (2× Rattata Lv. 9 · Zubat Lv. 9 + Ekans Lv. 11 · Rattata Lv. 7 + 2× Zubat Lv. 9), then their leader Proton (Koffing Lv. 14).

Back on his feet, Kurt hands you a Lure Ball on request and explains his craft: bring him Apricorns (seven kinds) and he turns them into specialized Poké Balls. Start collecting from every Apricorn tree you pass — a big enough batch handed over at once pays off.

SET-STYLE CAP — Bugsy: for 'Did Someone Call an Exterminator?', fight in the Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 16 during the battle, no Pack items.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Bugsy — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Gym trainers among the bushes: Twins Amy & May (Ledyba Lv. 10, Spinarak Lv. 10), Bug Catcher Al (Caterpie Lv. 12, Weedle Lv. 12), Bug Catcher Benny (Weedle Lv. 7, Kakuna Lv. 9, Beedrill Lv. 12), Bug Catcher Josh (Paras Lv. 13).
Bugsy: Metapod Lv. 14, Kakuna Lv. 14, Scyther Lv. 16. Win the Hive Badge and TM49 Fury Cutter.
Your rival blocks the way out toward Ilex Forest: Gastly Lv. 12, Zubat Lv. 14, and his starter — now evolved.
In Ilex Forest, the charcoal apprentice's Farfetch'd has wandered off. Herd it back by circling around it; the kiln owner rewards you with HM01 Cut. Teach it and cut your way onward.
The man on the eastern ledge hands over TM02 Headbutt. Teach it and start headbutting trees — sooner or later something is asleep up there.

Route 34 north: Youngster Samuel (Rattata Lv. 7, Sandshrew Lv. 10, 2× Spearow Lv. 8), Pokéfan Brandon (Snubbull Lv. 13 holding a Berry), Picnicker Gina (2× Hoppip Lv. 9, Bulbasaur Lv. 12 — register her, she occasionally hands over a Leaf Stone), Youngster Ian (Mankey Lv. 10, Diglett Lv. 12), Officer Keith (Growlithe Lv. 17, night only), Camper Todd (Psyduck Lv. 14). The gate woman gives TM12 Sweet Scent. The Surf-side trainers (Irene, Jenn, Kate) and the fenced Nugget and Rare Candy wait for a later pass. Grass: Pidgey, Rattata, Jigglypuff, Abra, Drowzee, Hoothoot, Snubbull (Lv. 10–12).

At the Day Care, the couple raises Pokémon ($100 flat + $100 per level gained; compatible pairs produce Eggs, Ditto substitutes). The Day-Care Man hands you the Odd Egg — carry it; what hatches stays a mystery until it does. A hidden Super Potion sits in the enclosure. Goldenrod City is next.

chapters.title: Goldenrod City
chapters.intro: The big city: six floors of shopping, a casino, a radio tower — and Whitney's Miltank waiting at the end of it.

steps.text: Goldenrod City. The Department Store: 2F Trainer's Market (standard balls/heals), 3F battle items ($350–$950), 4F vitamins ($9,800 each), 5F TM Corner (Fire/Ice/ThunderPunch $3,000, TM02 Headbutt $2,000), 6F vending machines ($200–$350). A 5F trade NPC swaps a Machop for an Abra. On Sundays a vendor gives TM27 Return if your lead Pokémon loves you — TM21 Frustration if it doesn't.
Occasionally a Rooftop Sale opens on the roof — Camper Todd calls from Route 34 when it starts (register him!). Buy anything up there: discounted Poké Balls ($150) and healing items.
The Bike Shop owner loans you a bicycle to drum up publicity. Ride it around; he calls later to confirm business improved — the bike is yours to keep.

The Underground tunnel: pick up the Coin Case here. Haircut brothers groom a Pokémon's friendship (younger: $300 on Sun/Wed/Fri; older: $500 on Tue/Thu/Sat); a bargain stall opens Mondays 4–9 AM (Nuggets, pearls, stardust); an herb shop trades cheap, bitter medicine on weekends.

The Game Corner (Coin Case required): coins at 50/$1,000 or 500/$10,000. Prizes: TM25 Thunder, TM14 Blizzard, TM38 Fire Blast (5,500 coins each), Abra Lv. 5 (100), Cubone Lv. 15 (800), Wobbuffet Lv. 15 (1,500). Spin the slots — two 7s lined up shows Golem, three pays the jackpot — and try the Card Flip table for a x24 win. Outside, keep an eye out for Bill's father, who teaches a move.

Radio Tower 1F quiz (answers: Yes, Yes, No, Yes, No) wins the Radio Card for your Pokégear. On 2F, Buena hands out a Blue Card — tune into her show for the daily password and bank 30 points for her prizes (and her phone number).

Also in town: the Name Rater (renames only Pokémon you caught yourself), the Magnet Train station (service delayed — you'll ride it much later), and the Flower Shop, where the woman frets about an odd tree on Route 36 that dodges water. Remember her after the badge.
SET-STYLE CAP — Whitney: for 'Attack of the Mad Cow', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 20 during the battle, no Pack items.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Whitney — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Gym trainers: Beauty Victoria (Sentret Lv. 9, 13, 17), Beauty Samantha (2× Meowth Lv. 16), Lass Carrie (Snubbull Lv. 18), Lass Bridget (3× Jigglypuff Lv. 15).

Whitney: Clefairy Lv. 18 (Encore, DoubleSlap, Mimic, Metronome) and the infamous Miltank Lv. 20 (Stomp, Attract, Milk Drink, Rollout). She bursts into tears when she loses — talk to her again for the Plain Badge (Speed boost, Strength in the field), TM45 Attract, and $2,000.
Somewhere around now, the Egg from Elm's aide hatches into Togepi. Fly to New Bark Town and show the newborn to Professor Elm — he hands over an Everstone for the proof.

chapters.title: National Park & the Road to Ecruteak
chapters.intro: Bug-catching on contest days, a tree that hates water, and apricorns all the way to Ecruteak.

steps.text: Route 35 north: Picnicker Kim (Vulpix Lv. 15), Camper Elliot (Sandshrew Lv. 13, Marill Lv. 15), Picnicker Brooke (Pikachu Lv. 16), Camper Ivan (Diglett Lv. 10, Zubat Lv. 10, Diglett Lv. 14), Juggler Irwin (Voltorb Lv. 2/6/10/14), Firebreather Walt (Magmar Lv. 11, 13), Officer Dirk at night (2× Growlithe Lv. 14), Bug Catcher Arnie (Venonat Lv. 15), Bird Keeper Bryan (Pidgey Lv. 12, Pidgeotto Lv. 14). Pick up TM04 Rollout. The gatehouse guard hands you Kenya the Spearow with Flower Mail — deliver it to the man on Route 31 for TM50, and an HP Up awaits after the delivery.

National Park. On Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays the Bug-Catching Contest runs: 20 Park Balls, keep one catch, judged on rarity and condition. Prizes: Sun Stone (1st), Everstone (2nd), Gold Berry (3rd), Berry (runner-up). Win it once; win it again without catching a Butterfree, Beedrill, Scyther, or Pinsir; and once more with Cooltrainer Nick among the contestants. On non-contest days, trainers roam: Pokéfan Beverly (Snubbull Lv. 14 — register her), Pokéfan William (Raichu Lv. 14), Schoolboy Jack (Oddish Lv. 12, Voltorb Lv. 15), Lass Krise (Oddish Lv. 12, Cubone Lv. 15). Items: Quick Claw, Paralyze Heal, TM28 Dig, hidden Full Heal, Nugget.

Route 36: Psychic Mark (2× Abra Lv. 13, Kadabra Lv. 15), Schoolboy Alan (Tangela Lv. 16); Ice Berry tree (daily), Hard Stone, Fire Stone. The odd tree blocks the crossroads: fetch the SquirtBottle from the Goldenrod Flower Shop (now that you hold the Plain Badge) and water it — Sudowoodo, Lv. 20 (Rock Throw, Mimic, Flail, Low Kick), attacks. CATCH it and register it; this is your one shot at it. TM08 Rock Smash opens up afterwards.

missable.deadline: One chance: catch Sudowoodo when you water it — the RA set flags this catch as missable

steps.text: Route 37: Twins Ann & Anne (Clefairy Lv. 16, Jigglypuff Lv. 16), Psychic Greg (Drowzee Lv. 17). Red, Blue, and Black Apricorn trees grow here (daily — Kurt is waiting), plus a hidden Ether and a Magnet. Ecruteak City is just ahead.

chapters.title: Ecruteak City
chapters.intro: A city of ghosts and legends: the Kimono Girls hold Surf, and whatever sleeps under the Burned Tower is about to wake up.

steps.text: In the Ecruteak Pokémon Center, Bill is finishing the Time Capsule — trades with Generation I games open up 24 hours later.

At the Dance Theater, the five Kimono Girls each fight with one Eevee evolution at Lv. 17: Naoko (Flareon), Sayo (Espeon), Zuki (Umbreon), Kuni (Vaporeon), Miki (Jolteon) — $1,224 each. Beat all five for HM03 Surf (field use unlocks with the Fog Badge).

Inside the Burned Tower, Eusine is hunting traces of Suicune — and your rival is hunting you. His team varies with your starter (against Chikorita: Haunter Lv. 20, Zubat Lv. 20, Magnemite Lv. 18, Quilava Lv. 22; $1,320).

Drop to the basement: the three legendary beasts stir. Two bolt instantly; Suicune pauses, looks at you, and is gone. Raikou and Entei now roam Johto's grass (their hunt is a late-game chapter); Suicune leaves sightings across the region — Eusine will keep crossing your path.
SET-STYLE CAP — Morty: for 'In the Spirit of Battle', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 25 during the battle, no Pack items.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Morty — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: The gym floor is a void crossed by an invisible bridge — stray and you restart at the entrance; follow each trainer's line of sight. Sage Ping (5× Gastly Lv. 16), Medium Grace (2× Haunter Lv. 20), Sage Jeffrey (Haunter Lv. 22), Medium Martha (Gastly Lv. 18, Haunter Lv. 20, Gastly Lv. 20).
Morty: Gastly Lv. 21, Haunter Lv. 21, Gengar Lv. 25, Haunter Lv. 23. The Fog Badge makes Surf usable in the field — Johto's water just opened — plus TM30 Shadow Ball.

chapters.title: Olivine & Cianwood
chapters.intro: A sick Ampharos darkens the lighthouse, the gym leader is off nursing it, and the medicine is across the sea.

steps.text: West onto Route 38: Beauty Valerie (Hoppip Lv. 17, Skiploom Lv. 17), Schoolboy Chad (Mr. Mime Lv. 19). Register Lass Dana — she gifts a Thunder Stone by phone. A Berry tree grows daily.

Route 39: Pokéfan Derek and Pokéfan Ruth (Pikachu Lv. 17 each, holding Berries — register Derek, his calls pay off in gold later), Sailor Eugene (Poliwhirl Lv. 17, Krabby Lv. 19); Mint Berry tree daily. At Moomoo Farm a Miltank lies sick: feed it seven Berries to nurse it back — TM13 Snore is your thanks, and Moomoo Milk goes on sale at $500.
Olivine City: the fisherman south of the Gym hands over the Good Rod; a sailor in the café teaches HM04 Strength. The Gym stands empty — Jasmine is up the lighthouse with a patient.

Climb the Lighthouse. 2F: Gentleman Alfred (Noctowl Lv. 20), Sailor Huey (Poliwag Lv. 18, Poliwhirl Lv. 18). 3F: Bird Keeper Theo (5× Pidgey Lv. 15–19), Gentleman Preston (2× Growlithe Lv. 18), Sailor Terrell (Poliwhirl Lv. 20). 4F: Lass Connie (Marill Lv. 21), Sailor Kent (2× Krabby Lv. 18–20). 5F: Bird Keeper Denis (2× Spearow Lv. 18, Fearow Lv. 20), Sailor Ernest (2× Machop Lv. 18, Poliwhirl Lv. 18). At the beacon, Jasmine tends the critically ill Ampharos, Amphy — she needs medicine from the Cianwood pharmacy.

Surf out onto Route 40: Swimmer Simon (2× Tentacool Lv. 20), Swimmer Randall (2× Shellder Lv. 18, Wartortle Lv. 20), Swimmer Paula (Staryu Lv. 19, Shellder Lv. 19), Swimmer Elaine (Staryu Lv. 21). A hidden Hyper Potion needs Rock Smash; Monica hands out a Sharp Beak.

Route 41's open sea is packed with swimmers: George (six Pokémon), Kara, Mathew, Denise, Kirk, Berke, Charlie, Susie, Kaylee, Wendy (water types, Lv. 18–23). The Whirl Islands churn here — their caves need Whirlpool, a later expedition.

In Cianwood City, the centuries-old pharmacy hands over the SecretPotion once you explain the lighthouse situation. Nearby, a PokéManiac — bullied by someone who sounds a lot like your rival — asks you to safeguard his Shuckle, 'Shuckie'. Take it: raise its friendship and he lets you keep it for good, and a Shuckle holding a Berry can turn it into Berry Juice after a battle.

missable.deadline: Accept Shuckie now and raise its friendship before returning it — Mania's permission is the missable part

steps.text: Suicune appears on the northern beach — and Eusine wants answers: he battles you with Drowzee Lv. 23, Haunter Lv. 23, Electrode Lv. 25 ($2,500).

missable.deadline: Before moving on from Cianwood — the RA set flags Eusine's battle as missable

steps.text: SET-STYLE CAP — Chuck: for 'Black Belt in Training', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 30 during the battle, no Pack items.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Chuck — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Use Strength to shove the three boulders clear, through Blackbelt Yoshi (Hitmonlee Lv. 27), Blackbelt Lao (Hitmonchan Lv. 27), Blackbelt Nob (Machop Lv. 25, Machoke Lv. 25), Blackbelt Lung (2× Mankey Lv. 23, Primeape Lv. 25). Chuck: Primeape Lv. 27 (Leer, Rage, Karate Chop, Fury Swipes) and Poliwrath Lv. 30 (Hypnosis, Mind Reader, Surf, DynamicPunch — Mind Reader makes the next DynamicPunch unmissable). Storm Badge + TM01 DynamicPunch; outside, Chuck's wife teaches HM02 Fly.
Fly back to Olivine and climb to the beacon: the SecretPotion cures Amphy, the light returns, and Jasmine heads down to her Gym.
SET-STYLE CAP — Jasmine: for 'Stainless Stealing Victory', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 35 during the battle, no Pack items.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Jasmine — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Jasmine, alone in her Gym: 2× Magnemite Lv. 30 and Steelix Lv. 35 — Fire and Water moves crack the steel. Mineral Badge + TM23 Iron Tail.

East of Ecruteak, Route 42: Suicune flashes through the Apricorn grove — your second sighting. Mt. Mortar's surface path passes PokéManiac Miller and Super Nerd Markus; its depths (Surf, Strength, Waterfall — Nugget and Escape Rope below) are a later expedition. Mahogany Town lies east.

missable.deadline: The Route 42 Suicune sighting — the RA set flags this encounter as missable

chapters.title: Mahogany & Lake of Rage
chapters.intro: Something at the lake is screaming on a frequency only Magikarp can hear — and the souvenir shop has a basement.

steps.text: Mahogany Town: a pushy salesman hawks RageCandyBars at $300, and a man blocks the Gym door, waving you north to the Lake of Rage.

Route 43 north: Camper Spencer (Sandshrew Lv. 17, Sandslash Lv. 17, Zubat Lv. 19), Picnicker Tiffany (Clefairy Lv. 20), Fisher Marvin (Magikarp Lv. 10/15, Gyarados Lv. 10/15), PokéManiac Ron (Nidoking Lv. 19), PokéManiac Brent (Lickitung Lv. 19), PokéManiac Ben (Slowbro Lv. 19). Rocket Grunts in the gate shake you down for a $1,000 'toll' — the grass path around avoids it.

The Lake of Rage's centerpiece: the red Gyarados, Lv. 30 (Thrash, Bite, Dragon Rage, Leer). CATCH it — a shiny you are guaranteed exactly once — and register it. The Red Scale lands in your bag after the battle either way, and Lance introduces himself: Team Rocket is forcing Magikarp to evolve with a radio signal from Mahogany.

missable.deadline: One guaranteed shiny: catch (don't KO) the red Gyarados — the RA set flags this catch as missable

steps.text: Around the lake: the Fishing Guru in the lakeside house collects record Magikarp — break his size record and he pays in Elixirs. Wesley waits down the dead-end path west of the secluded house.
Quick detour: Fly south and walk to Mr. Pokémon on Route 30 — he trades your Red Scale for the EXP Share.

Back in Mahogany, Lance has opened the way: the souvenir shop hides a staircase. B1F: a Grunt with 4× Rattata Lv. 16, Scientist Jed (3× Magnemite Lv. 20), and the two security Grunts (Drowzee Lv. 17 + Zubat Lv. 19; Zubat Lv. 16 + Grimer Lv. 17 + Rattata Lv. 18).

Deeper: Grunts (2× Venonat Lv. 18 · Golbat Lv. 18 · Rattata Lv. 17 + Zubat Lv. 17 + Rattata Lv. 17 · Ekans Lv. 18 + Gloom Lv. 18 · Raticate Lv. 19), Scientist Ross (2× Koffing Lv. 22), Scientist Mitch (Ditto Lv. 24). The door passwords are 'SlowpokeTail' and 'RaticateTail'; Executive Petrel guards them (Zubat Lv. 22, Koffing Lv. 22, Raticate Lv. 24).

Executive Ariana makes her stand (Arbok Lv. 23, Gloom Lv. 23, Murkrow Lv. 25). With Lance at your side, shut down the three Electrode Lv. 23 powering the broadcast — defeat or catch them. The signal dies; Lance rewards you with HM06 Whirlpool. Sweep every trainer in the hideout before you leave.

SET-STYLE CAP — Pryce: for 'Braving a Brutal Blizzard', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 31 during the battle, no Pack items. Mind the order: this cap (31) is LOWER than Jasmine's (35) relative to where you are.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Pryce — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Slide the spin-ice floor to reach the trainers: Skier Roxanne (Jynx Lv. 28), Boarder Ronald (2× Seel Lv. 24, Dewgong Lv. 25), Skier Clarissa (Dewgong Lv. 28), Boarder Brad (2× Swinub Lv. 26), Boarder Douglas (2× Shellder Lv. 24, Cloyster Lv. 25). Pryce: Seel Lv. 27 (Headbutt, Icy Wind, Aurora Beam, Rest), Dewgong Lv. 29 (same set), Piloswine Lv. 31 (Icy Wind, Fury Attack, Mist, Blizzard). Glacier Badge, TM16 Icy Wind, $3,100 — and Elm calls in a panic: the Goldenrod Radio Tower has been seized.

chapters.title: Radio Tower & Tin Tower
chapters.intro: Team Rocket has the airwaves and a fake director on the fifth floor. Win the tower back, and a bell rings for the north wind.

steps.text: Fly to Goldenrod. The Radio Tower crawls with Rockets: a Grunt with 2× Raticate Lv. 24 on the ground floor, four more Grunts on 2F (one fields 5× Rattata, Lv. 21–23), Scientist Marc on 3F (3× Magnemite Lv. 27) among others. Fight upward.
The 'Director' on 5F is Executive Petrel in disguise: 5× Koffing Lv. 30 and Weezing Lv. 32 ($2,160). He drops the Basement Key — the real director is under the city.

In the Underground's B2F switch maze, flip #3, #2, #1 to open the way through (the #2, #1, #2, #3 sequence reaches the item stash: Full Heal, Max Potion, Revive, Smoke Ball). Your rival picks this moment: Golbat Lv. 30, Magnemite Lv. 28, Haunter Lv. 30, Sneasel Lv. 32, and his starter at Lv. 32. In the warehouse, the real Director hands you the Card Key.

Card Key in hand, retake the tower: Proton on 4F (Golbat Lv. 36), Ariana on 5F (Arbok, Vileplume, Murkrow — all Lv. 32), and finally Archer (Houndour Lv. 33, Koffing Lv. 33, Houndoom Lv. 35). Every Executive falls — and for the clean sweep, every Grunt and Scientist in the tower and the Underground too.
The grateful Director gives you the Clear Bell; the staff add TM11 Sunny Day and a Pink Bow. Team Rocket's broadcast dream is over — and the bell wants to be rung in Ecruteak.
On your way back through Route 36, Suicune appears a third time — closer, calmer, watching you before it flees.

missable.deadline: The Route 36 Suicune sighting — the RA set flags this encounter as missable

steps.text: At Ecruteak's Tin Tower, the Wise Trio bars the door, satisfied only by the Clear Bell and your record with Suicune: Sage Gaku (Noctowl Lv. 32 + Flareon), Sage Masa (Noctowl Lv. 32 + Jolteon), Sage Koji (Noctowl Lv. 32 + Vaporeon).

On the Tin Tower's ground floor, Suicune finally turns to fight: Lv. 40 — Leer, BubbleBeam, Rain Dance, Gust. Catch the north wind. For 'Crystalline Catch', do it WITHOUT the Master Ball (save your single Master Ball — Elm's gift comes with all 8 badges — for nothing, ideally).

missable.deadline: Catch Suicune without using the Master Ball — using it forfeits 'Crystalline Catch'

chapters.title: Ice Path & Blackthorn
chapters.intro: Across the ice to the dragon city — where the badge isn't won in the gym, but under it.

steps.text: Route 44, a mountain path of springs and ponds: Psychic Phil (Natu Lv. 24, Kadabra Lv. 26), Fisher Edgar (2× Remoraid Lv. 25), Cooltrainer Cybil (Butterfree Lv. 25, Bellossom Lv. 25), Cooltrainer Allen (Charmeleon Lv. 27), PokéManiac Zach (Rhyhorn Lv. 27), Fisher Wilton (2× Goldeen Lv. 23, Seaking Lv. 25 — register him), Bird Keeper Vance (2× Pidgeotto Lv. 25 — register him too; both call with rematches and gifts).

Into the Ice Path. Slide the ice and shove boulders into holes to descend. Grab HM07 Waterfall on the far side of the third ice patch on 1F (plus a PP Up below); B1F holds an Iron and a hidden Max Potion mid-puzzle; B2F a Full Heal, TM44 Rest, hidden Carbos and Ice Heal; B3F a Never-Melt Ice past the breakable rock. The cave's wilds: Zubat, Golbat, Jynx, Swinub, Delibird, and (below 1F) Sneasel, Lv. 20–26.
Blackthorn City: the girl in the eastern house trades a Dodrio for your female Dragonair; the Move Deleter in the southern house erases moves for free (the only way to unlearn an HM move).

SET-STYLE CAP — Clair: for 'Down to the Last Breath', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 40 during the battle, no Pack items — and no Legendaries other than the Beasts (your Suicune is legal).

missable.deadline: Before defeating Clair — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Push boulders into the lava to bridge the gym: Cooltrainer Paul (3× Dratini Lv. 34), Cooltrainer Fran (Seadra Lv. 37), Cooltrainer Cody (Horsea Lv. 34, Seadra Lv. 36), Cooltrainer Mike (Dragonair Lv. 37), Cooltrainer Lola (Dratini Lv. 34, Dragonair Lv. 36). Clair: 3× Dragonair Lv. 37 (Thunder Wave, DragonBreath, and one each of Surf / Thunderbolt / Ice Beam) and Kingdra Lv. 40 (Surf, DragonBreath, SmokeScreen, Hyper Beam). She loses — and refuses you the badge until the Dragon's Den says otherwise.

Surf and Whirlpool through the cave behind the gym into the Dragon's Den. At the Dragon Shrine, answer the elder's quiz: Pal/Friend · Strategy/Raising · Tough person/Anybody · Love/Knowledge · 'Both'. Clair concedes the Rising Badge and TM24 DragonBreath. Answer EVERY question right and the elder also gifts a Dratini that knows ExtremeSpeed — get one answer wrong and it knows plain Leer instead.

missable.deadline: Answer all shrine questions correctly the first time — the ExtremeSpeed Dratini is decided then and there

steps.text: Optional sweep south: Route 45 and Route 46 hold Hiker Erik, Cooltrainers Ryan and Kelly, Black Belt Kenji (register him — his calls pay off in PP Ups), and others (Lv. 13–30), with Dark Cave's chambers between them. Clear what you can reach; the cave's far side needs Flash and Surf.

All 8 Johto badges! Stop by Elm's lab: the Master Ball is yours (remember — 'Crystalline Catch'-style achievements want it unused). Heal up; east of New Bark, the water route to Kanto's Victory Road awaits.

chapters.title: Road to the League
chapters.intro: East over the water, up Tohjo Falls, and through a cave with no trainers — your rival saved himself for the door.

steps.text: Surf east from New Bark onto Route 27 and climb Tohjo Falls with Waterfall. Trainers: Cooltrainer Megan (Bulbasaur Lv. 32, Ivysaur Lv. 32, Venusaur Lv. 32), Cooltrainer Blake (Magneton Lv. 33, Quagsire Lv. 31, Exeggcute Lv. 31), Bird Keeper Jose (Farfetch'd Lv. 34), Cooltrainer Brian (Sandslash Lv. 35), Psychic Gilbert (Starmie Lv. 30, Exeggcute Lv. 30, Girafarig Lv. 34), Cooltrainer Reena (Starmie Lv. 31, Nidoqueen Lv. 33, Starmie Lv. 31). Items: Rare Candy, TM22 SolarBeam, TM37 Sandstorm, Star Piece.

Route 26 north: Fisher Scott (2× Qwilfish Lv. 30, Seaking Lv. 34), Psychic Richard (Espeon Lv. 36), Cooltrainer Joyce (Pikachu Lv. 36, Blastoise Lv. 32), Cooltrainer Gaven (Victreebel Lv. 32, Kingler Lv. 32, Flareon Lv. 32), Cooltrainer Jake (Parasect Lv. 33, Golduck Lv. 35), Cooltrainer Beth (Rapidash Lv. 36). A rest-stop house heals your team; the Week Siblings' residence stands here too (their seven day-by-day gifts are a long-haul project). Items: Ice Berry, Max Elixir. The League Reception Gate checks your badges.

Victory Road: five floors, no trainers — just wilds and loot (2× Full Heal, Max Revive, HP Up, Full Restore, TM26 Earthquake, Max Potion). At the exit, your rival makes his last stand before the League: Sneasel Lv. 34, Golbat Lv. 36, Magneton Lv. 35, Haunter Lv. 35, Kadabra Lv. 35, and his starter's final form at Lv. 38.

chapters.title: Pokémon League
chapters.intro: Five doors, one save prompt each — and a crown at the end.

steps.text: Indigo Plateau: Pokémon Center and Mart (Ultra Ball $1,200, Max Potion $2,500, Revive $1,500, Full Restore $3,000). Stock deep — the Elite Four is one continuous gauntlet.

SET-STYLE CAP — the League: for 'Overcoming the Heavenly Kings', clear the Elite Four AND Lance in a single session, Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 44 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts. And for 'Struggle to the Top', a separate run: beat them all using only a single Pokémon.

missable.deadline: Decided across one full League session — plan the run before stepping in

steps.text: Will, Psychic: Xatu Lv. 40, Exeggutor Lv. 41, Slowbro Lv. 41, Jynx Lv. 41, Xatu Lv. 42 ($4,200). Koga, Poison: Ariados Lv. 40, Venomoth Lv. 41, Forretress Lv. 43, Muk Lv. 42, Crobat Lv. 44 ($4,400). Bruno, Fighting: Hitmontop Lv. 42, Hitmonlee Lv. 42, Hitmonchan Lv. 42, Onix Lv. 43, Machamp Lv. 46 ($4,600). Karen, Dark: Umbreon Lv. 42, Vileplume Lv. 42, Murkrow Lv. 44, Gengar Lv. 45, Houndoom Lv. 47 ($4,700).

Champion Lance: Gyarados Lv. 44, Dragonite Lv. 47, Charizard Lv. 46, Aerodactyl Lv. 46, Dragonite Lv. 47, Dragonite Lv. 50 ($5,000). Win, and the Hall of Fame takes your team — roll credits. Back home, Professor Elm hands over the S.S. Ticket: Kanto is open.

chapters.title: Kanto East
chapters.intro: Three years after Red, Kanto runs on a broken Power Plant and a stolen Machine Part. Fix the region, badge by badge.

steps.text: Board the S.S. Aqua at Olivine Harbor. A Gentleman's granddaughter has gone missing — search the decks and find her in the Captain's cabin for a Metal Coat. First-voyage trainers: Sailor Stanly (Machop, Machoke, Psyduck Lv. 26–33), Hiker Noland (Sandslash, Golem Lv. 31–33), Pokéfan Colin (Delibird Lv. 32), Twins Meg & Peg (Phanpy, Teddiursa Lv. 31), Firebreather Lyle (Koffing, Flareon, Koffing Lv. 28–31), Juggler Fritz (Mr. Mime, Magmar, Machoke Lv. 29), Sailor Jeff (2× Raticate Lv. 32), Picnicker Debra (Seaking Lv. 33). The full every-voyage sweep is an endgame project. Dock at Vermilion City.

Vermilion City: a Snorlax snores in front of Diglett's Cave — nothing wakes it yet. At the Pokémon Fan Club, sit through the Chairman's Rapidash saga for a Rare Candy (remember this building). A Full Heal hides in the bush by the Gym.
SET-STYLE CAP — Lt. Surge: for 'Shock to the System', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 46 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Lt. Surge — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: The trash-can switches are inactive — walk straight to the Lightning American. Lt. Surge: Raichu Lv. 44 (Thunderbolt, Thunder, Thunder Wave, Quick Attack), Magneton Lv. 40 (Zap Cannon, Lock-On, Double Team, Swift), 2× Electrode Lv. 40 (Screech, Double Team, Swift, Explosion), Electabuzz Lv. 46 (ThunderPunch, Thunder, Light Screen, Quick Attack). Thunder Badge + $4,600.

North through Route 6 (Pokéfan Rex: Phanpy Lv. 35; Pokéfan Allan: Teddiursa Lv. 35 — both holding Berries) into Saffron City. Mr. Psychic in the southeast house hands over TM29 Psychic; the Silph Co. guard gifts an Up-Grade; a Focus Band sits at the back of the leaderless Fighting Dojo. The Magnet Train sits dead — the Power Plant is the reason.
SET-STYLE CAP — Sabrina: for 'Telepathic Miscommunications', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 48 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Sabrina — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Warp through the teleporter maze: Medium Rebecca (Drowzee Lv. 35, Hypno Lv. 35), Psychic Jared (Mr. Mime Lv. 32, 2× Exeggcute Lv. 32/35), Medium Doris (Slowpoke Lv. 34, Slowbro Lv. 36), Psychic Franklin (Kadabra Lv. 37). Sabrina: Espeon Lv. 46 (Sand-Attack, Quick Attack, Swift, Psychic), Mr. Mime Lv. 46 (Barrier, Reflect, Baton Pass, Psychic), Alakazam Lv. 48 (Recover, Future Sight, Psychic, Reflect). Marsh Badge + $4,800.

East on Route 8: Biker Dwayne (4× Koffing Lv. 27–30), Biker Harris (Flareon Lv. 34), Biker Zeke (2× Koffing Lv. 32), Super Nerd Sam (Grimer, Muk Lv. 34), Super Nerd Tom (3× Magnemite Lv. 32). Lavender Town keeps its Soul House and a free Name Rater; the Kanto Radio Tower's upper programs need an EXPN Card you don't have yet. Through Rock Tunnel (Flash; TM47 Steel Wing, Elixir, Max Potion, PP Up, Revive, Iron inside) past Route 9's hikers and campers (Edna, Sid, Dean, Tim, Heidi, Sidney — Lv. 29–34) to Route 10. A trader swaps your Dugtrio for a Magneton holding a Metal Coat.
At the Power Plant, the Manager despairs: the generator's Machine Part was stolen. Word is a shady character was seen near Cerulean City.

In Cerulean Gym, a Team Rocket holdout flees when confronted. Chase him to Route 24: the Grunt (Golbat Lv. 30) confesses — the Machine Part is hidden in the Cerulean Gym's waters. Dive back and fish it out of the enclosed pool. (A Berserk Gene hides off Route 24's river, Surf required.)
Return the Machine Part: the generator hums, the Manager hands over TM07 Zap Cannon — and Kanto's electricity, radio, and Magnet Train come back to life.

The gratitude tour: the Copycat in Saffron lost her Poké Doll — retrieve the Clefairy Doll from the Vermilion Fan Club and return it for the Pass (free Magnet Train rides!). Then the Lavender Radio Tower's manager rewards the region's savior with the EXPN Card. En route, the Underground Path hides a Full Restore and an X Special; the woman in the old Route 5 Day-Care house gives a Cleanse Tag.

Route 25's trainer gauntlet: Schoolboy Dudley (Oddish Lv. 35), Lass Ellen (Wigglytuff Lv. 30, Granbull Lv. 34), Schoolboy Joe (Tangela, Vaporeon Lv. 33), Lass Laura (Gloom Lv. 28, Pidgeotto Lv. 31, Bellossom Lv. 31), Camper Lloyd (Nidoking Lv. 34), Lass Shannon (2× Paras Lv. 29, Parasect Lv. 32), Super Nerd Pat (Porygon Lv. 36), Cooltrainer Kevin (Rhyhorn Lv. 38, Charmeleon Lv. 35, Wartortle Lv. 35 — pays a Nugget). At Bill's house, his grandfather asks to see five Pokémon: Lickitung → Everstone, Oddish → Leaf Stone, Staryu → Water Stone, Growlithe → Fire Stone, Pichu → Thunder Stone. On the cape, Misty is mid-date — interrupting it sends her back to her Gym.
SET-STYLE CAP — Misty: for 'Poor, Washed-Up Mermaid', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 47 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Misty — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Swim past Swimmer Parker (2× Horsea Lv. 32, Seadra Lv. 35), Swimmer Briana (2× Seaking Lv. 35), Swimmer Diana (Golduck Lv. 37). Misty: Golduck Lv. 42 (Surf, Disable, Psych Up, Psychic), Quagsire Lv. 42 (Surf, Amnesia, Earthquake, Rain Dance), Lapras Lv. 44 (Surf, Perish Song, Blizzard, Rain Dance), Starmie Lv. 47 (Surf, Confuse Ray, Recover, Ice Beam). Cascade Badge + $4,700.

chapters.title: Kanto West
chapters.intro: Five more badges around the loop — and at the end of it, the man who was Champion before you were ever asked.

steps.text: West through Route 7's grass into Celadon City. On the Mansion roof at night, an NPC teaches TM03 Curse; the restaurant's trash hides Leftovers; a Game Corner regular hands you 18 Coins; a hidden PP Up sits in the southeast dead-end tree. (After you've caught Suicune, Eusine turns up in the Pokémon Center, musing about Ho-Oh.)
SET-STYLE CAP — Erika: for 'Slicing the Blades of Yesterday', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 46 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Erika — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Through Twins Jo & Zoe, Picnicker Tanya, Lass Michelle, and Beauty Julia to Erika: Tangela Lv. 42 (Vine Whip, Bind, Giga Drain, Sleep Powder), Jumpluff Lv. 41 (Mega Drain, Leech Seed, Cotton Spore, Giga Drain), Victreebel Lv. 46 (Sunny Day, Synthesis, Acid, Razor Leaf), Bellossom Lv. 46 (Sunny Day, Synthesis, Petal Dance, SolarBeam). Rainbow Badge, TM19 Giga Drain, $4,600.

Coast down Cycling Road (Route 17): Biker Riley (Weezing Lv. 34), Biker Glenn (Koffing Lv. 28, Magmar Lv. 30, Weezing Lv. 32), Biker Joel (2× Magmar Lv. 32), Biker Charles (Koffing, Charmeleon, Weezing Lv. 30); hidden Max Elixir en route. Route 18: Bird Keeper Bob (Noctowl Lv. 34), Bird Keeper Boris (2× Doduo Lv. 28/30, Dodrio Lv. 32). Fuchsia City grows a Burnt Berry daily.

SET-STYLE CAP — Janine: for 'Separating the "Ill" from "Illusions"', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 39 during battle, no Pack items — and NO Legendaries at all (no Beast exception here).

missable.deadline: Before defeating Janine — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: The gym's glass panels hide the trainers — follow the east wall to the back, then loop to the front. Lass Linda (Bulbasaur Lv. 30, Ivysaur Lv. 32, Venusaur Lv. 34), Picnicker Cindy (Nidoqueen Lv. 36), Camper Barry (Nidoking Lv. 36), Lass Alice (2× Gloom Lv. 30, Arbok Lv. 34). Janine: Crobat Lv. 36 (Wing Attack, Confuse Ray, Supersonic, Screech), 2× Weezing Lv. 36 (Sludge Bomb, Smog, Toxic, Explosion), Ariados Lv. 33 (String Shot, Night Shade, Giga Drain, Scary Face), Venomoth Lv. 39 (Toxic, Psychic, Double Team, Supersonic). Soul Badge, TM06 Toxic, $3,900.

Loop back east along Routes 15, 14, 13, 12: schoolboys, teachers, bird keepers, and Pokéfans (Lv. 23–36 — including Pokéfan Joshua's six Lv. 23 Pikachu and Teacher Hillary's Aipom/Cubone). On Route 14, a trader swaps your Chansey for an Aerodactyl holding a Gold Berry; on Route 12, the Fishing Guru's younger brother hands over the SUPER ROD — the third and last rod. Items: PP Up (Cut), hidden Calcium, Nugget and Elixir off Route 12's water.

Back at Vermilion's Diglett's Cave entrance: tune the Pokégear radio to CHANNEL 20.0 — the Poké Flute — and nudge the Snorlax. Lv. 50, holding Leftovers (Snore, Rest, Body Slam, Rollout). CATCH it and register it — there is exactly one.

missable.deadline: One Snorlax: catch (don't KO) it when you wake it — the RA set flags this catch as missable

steps.text: Through Diglett's Cave (hidden Max Revive at the north end) to western Kanto. Route 2's bug catchers (Rob: Beedrill + Butterfree Lv. 32; Doug: Ariados Lv. 34; Ed: 3× Beedrill Lv. 30) guard a stash: Carbos, Elixir, Dire Hit, Full Restore, Revive, Max Ether, Full Heal, Max Potion, plus a Nugget from the house NPC. In Pewter City, an elderly man reminiscing about Johto presses the SILVER WING into your hand (Lugia's door key), and the Pokémon Center hosts a Haunter → Xatu trade.
SET-STYLE CAP — Brock: for 'Gneiss Try, Mr. Jelly Donut!', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 44 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Brock — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Past Camper Jerry (Sandslash Lv. 37), Brock fights in order: Graveler Lv. 41 (Defense Curl, Rock Slide, Rollout, Earthquake), Rhyhorn Lv. 41 (Fury Attack, Scary Face, Earthquake, Horn Drill), Omastar Lv. 42 (Bite, Surf, Protect, Spike Cannon), Onix Lv. 44 (Bind, Rock Slide, Bide, Sandstorm), Kabutops Lv. 42 (Slash, Surf, Endure, Giga Drain). Boulder Badge + $4,200.

Route 3 east: Youngster Warren (Fearow Lv. 35), Youngster Jimmy (Raticate, Arbok Lv. 33), Firebreathers Otis and Burt. In Mt. Moon, your rival demands one more: Sneasel Lv. 41, Golbat Lv. 42, Magneton Lv. 41, Gengar Lv. 43, Alakazam Lv. 43, and his starter's final form at Lv. 45 ($4,500).

Mt. Moon Square (shop 10:00–17:59: Fresh Water $200, Poké Doll $1,000…): between Monday 20:00 and Tuesday 4:00, two Clefairy dance around the big rock — witness the ritual, then Rock Smash the rock for a MOON STONE. Weekly, if you miss it.

Down Route 4 and Route 1 (Schoolboy Danny: Jynx, Electabuzz, Magmar Lv. 31; Cooltrainer Quinn: Ivysaur, Starmie Lv. 38; Bitter Berry daily). In Pallet Town, Red's mother waits alone; at Oak's lab the Professor rates your Pokédex. Daisy Oak grooms one Pokémon a day between 15:00 and 16:00 — friendship up, free of charge.

Surf south over Routes 21 and 20 (swimmers Lv. 26–34; Tangela and Mr. Mime in the island grass). Cinnabar Island is a volcano-gutted ruin — a hidden Rare Candy in the north dead-end — and Blue stands at its edge, contemplating what nature destroys. He'll be waiting at Viridian. Blaine's gym now squats inside the Seafoam Islands on Route 20.
SET-STYLE CAP — Blaine: for 'Frying Up the Egg-Head', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 50 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Blaine — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Blaine, in his cave gym: Magcargo Lv. 45 (Curse, Smog, Flamethrower, Rock Slide), Magmar Lv. 45 (ThunderPunch, Fire Punch, Sunny Day, Confuse Ray), Rapidash Lv. 50 (Quick Attack, Fire Spin, Fury Attack, Fire Blast). Volcano Badge + $5,000.
Viridian City: TM42 Dream Eater waits southwest (Cut or Surf), and the Trainer House hosts Cal once a day (Meganium, Typhlosion, Feraligatr — all Lv. 50, $5,000).
SET-STYLE CAP — Blue: for 'Until Next Time... Smell Ya Later!', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only, nothing above level 58 during battle, no Pack items, no Legendaries other than the Beasts.

missable.deadline: Before defeating Blue — the Set-style challenge is decided in that one battle

steps.text: Blue, no preamble, no gym trainers: Pidgeot Lv. 56 (Quick Attack, Whirlwind, Wing Attack, Mirror Move), Alakazam Lv. 54 (Disable, Recover, Psychic, Reflect), Rhydon Lv. 56 (Fury Attack, Sandstorm, Rock Slide, Earthquake), Gyarados Lv. 58 (Twister, Hydro Pump, Rain Dance, Hyper Beam), Exeggutor Lv. 58 (Sunny Day, Leech Seed, Egg Bomb, SolarBeam), Arcanine Lv. 58 (Roar, Swift, Flamethrower, ExtremeSpeed). Earth Badge + $5,800 — all 16. Professor Oak hears of it and opens Mt. Silver.

chapters.title: Legendary Roundup
chapters.intro: The wings are in your bag, the beasts are in the grass, and three old gods are waiting to see if you do this without the Master Ball.

steps.text: Raikou and Entei have roamed Johto's grass since the Burned Tower. Track them on the Pokédex area screen, lead with something fast that can trap or sleep, and whittle them down across encounters — they flee on sight. Catch both, and for the paired no-Master-Ball feats, catch each without it.

missable.deadline: Catch Raikou and Entei without the Master Ball — using it on either forfeits its no-Master-Ball achievement

steps.text: With the Silver Wing from Pewter, brave the Whirl Islands (Whirlpool + Flash): enter by the northeast island, descend to B1F, ride the B2F waterfall down, and take the west opening to the inner chamber. Lugia, Lv. 60 — Gust, Recover, Hydro Pump, Rain Dance. Catch the storm — and skip the Master Ball for 'Serenity Beyond the Storm'.

missable.deadline: Catch Lugia without the Master Ball for 'Serenity Beyond the Storm'

steps.text: All three beasts caught and the Hall of Fame behind you, the middle Sage on Tin Tower 1F bestows the Rainbow Wing. Climb to the top: Ho-Oh, Lv. 60 — Gust, Recover, Fire Blast, Sunny Day, holding Sacred Ash. The rainbow ends here — Master Ball pocketed, ideally.

missable.deadline: Catch Ho-Oh without the Master Ball for 'At the End of the Rainbow'

steps.text: Mt. Mortar's interior (Surf, Strength, Waterfall, Flash): TM40 Defense Curl, a Dragon Scale, Rare Candy, Elixir, and a Tyrogue (Lv. 10) for the taking in the depths.

Union Cave B2F, on a Friday: Lapras sings at Lv. 20 — catch and register it. The Surf-side Cooltrainers wait here too: Nick (Charmander, Bulbasaur, Squirtle — all Lv. 26), Gwen (Eevee Lv. 26 + an evolution Lv. 22), Emma (Poliwhirl Lv. 28).
Return to the Ruins of Alph with every field move: solve all the puzzle chambers, sweep every hidden item, and fill the Unown alphabet — all 26 forms.

chapters.title: Mt. Silver & Red
chapters.intro: Three years of silence at the top of a mountain. He doesn't say a word now either.

steps.text: Route 28 (the house lady gives TM47 Steel Wing; hidden Rare Candy and a Surf-side Full Restore) leads into Mt. Silver's cave: Golbat, Golduck, Graveler, Onix, Magmar, Ursaring at Lv. 42–53 — and Larvitar (Lv. 15–20) for your Pokédex. Items: Protein, Escape Rope, 2× Ultra Ball, Dire Hit, Max Elixir (1F); Max Potion, PP Up, Max Revive, Ultra Ball, Calcium, Full Restore (2F).

SET-STYLE CAP — Red: for 'To Train Them Is My Cause', Set battle style, Johto Pokémon only (no external trade-only Pokémon), nothing above level 65 during battle, no Pack items — ALL Johto Legendaries allowed this time. And 'A League of Your Own' wants him beaten with a single Pokémon (a separate climb).

missable.deadline: Decided in the one battle at the summit — plan the run before stepping up

steps.text: Red: Pikachu Lv. 81 (Charm, Quick Attack, Thunderbolt, Thunder), Espeon Lv. 73 (Mud-Slap, Swift, Reflect, Psychic), Snorlax Lv. 75 (Amnesia, Snore, Rest, Body Slam), Venusaur Lv. 77 (Sunny Day, Giga Drain, Synthesis, SolarBeam), Charizard Lv. 77 (Flamethrower, Wing Attack, Slash, Fire Spin), Blastoise Lv. 77 (Rain Dance, Blizzard, Surf, Whirlpool). $7,700, no words, credits. He returns to the summit after you re-clear the League.

chapters.title: Completion Roundup
chapters.intro: Everything the route only waved at, gathered for the long tail: the tower, the siblings, the dex.

steps.text: The Battle Tower on Route 40 runs 7-streak challenges: clear one for 'Game of Death-Defiance', then clear it again using Johto Pokémon only — no Lugia, no Ho-Oh — for 'Local Competition'.
The Week Siblings appear around Johto, one per day of the week (their family home stands on Route 26). Meet all seven on their days and collect all seven gifts.

Collect every in-game Gift Pokémon (the Odd Egg, Togepi's egg, Shuckie, the shrine Dratini, Tyrogue, Bill's Eevee, and the rest) and complete every in-game trade (Bellsprout→Onix, Abra→Machop, Dugtrio→Magneton, Haunter→Xatu, Chansey→Aerodactyl, Dragonair→Dodrio).
Defeat every non-missable trainer in Johto, then in Kanto. The rematch phone contacts you registered en route round out the list.
Collect every visible and hidden field item in Johto (RA flags the Johto sweep missable — some items sit behind one-time events) and in Kanto, and every finite TM and HM.

missable.deadline: The Johto item sweep is flagged missable by the RA set — mind one-time areas

steps.text: The registry pays out: a vitamin from every rematch trainer that rewards one, a PP Up from Blackbelt Kenji, a Nugget from Pokéfan Beverly or Derek, an Evolution Stone from a registered Pokégear trainer, and every Poké Doll Mom's savings can buy. Max a lead Pokémon's friendship for full-power Return — and deal super-effective damage with Hidden Power while you're at it.
Shake down the wilds: a Stick from a wild Farfetch'd, a Thick Club from a wild Cubone or Marowak, a Lucky Egg from a wild Chansey — catch or steal (Thief).
Ride the S.S. Aqua again (it alternates between Olivine and Vermilion) and defeat every possible trainer aboard, excluding those from the first voyage.
Your rival never stopped: win the optional rematch against him at Indigo Plateau.

The summit behind the summit: all 206 Pokémon obtainable in-game without external devices. The version exclusives, one-shot legendaries, and event windows along the route decide this one — the catch list widgets have tracked your debt since Route 29.

missable.deadline: 206 includes every one-shot catch on the route — each missed static is a hole this save cannot fill
